summary: Matched Peach's up-special implementation by moving a large block of `ftPe_Init.s` assembly into a new C file, `src/melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/ftPe_SpecialHi.c`, and wiring that object into `obj_files.mk`. The new C covers Peach Parasol entry/start/end anim/IASA/physics/collision paths plus item/parasol accessory callbacks. Supporting changes typed Peach fighter/dat/motion variables, added item prototypes, and performed broad mechanical naming cleanup for fighter input/co_attrs/math constants needed by the match. There were no PR comments or reviews in the slice, so reviewer rationale is unavailable.
what_changed: Removed roughly 1000 lines of Peach SpecialHi-related assembly from `asm/melee/ft/chara/ftPe_Init.s` while leaving the remaining assembly object in the build.

Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftPeach/ftPe_SpecialHi.c` with 395 lines implementing Peach Parasol/up-special behavior, including `ftPe_SpecialHi_Enter`, `ftPe_SpecialAirHi_Enter`, start/end Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll callbacks, and helper callbacks around the spawned parasol item.
Added `ftPe_SpecialHi.c.o` to `obj_files.mk` before the remaining `ftPe_Init.s.o`, so the newly matched C participates in the text object list.

Retyped Peach fighter variables at `fp+2238`/`fp+223C` as `HSD_GObj* unk_item_gobj` and `HSD_GObj* parasol_gobj` instead of raw `u32`, and added a `mv.pe.specialhi.kind` `ItemKind` motion-var view at `fp+2340`.
Expanded `ftPe_DatAttrs` fields around offsets `0x70` through `0x90` from an opaque byte array into named float/int fields used by SpecialHi physics, steering, and transitions.
Added or adjusted item-facing includes/prototypes such as `src/melee/it/it_27CF.h` to support calls like `it_802BDA64`, `it_802BDB94`, `it_802BDBF8`, `it_802BDC18`, and `it_802BDC38`.
Changed `src/math.h` degree/radian macros into `static float const deg_to_rad` and `rad_to_deg`, then mechanically updated many fighters to use `deg_to_rad` instead of `DEG_TO_RAD`.

Performed broad mechanical field-name cleanup in fighter code and many character files, e.g. input stick fields became `fp->input.lstick.x/y`, `held_inputs`, and common attributes became `fp->co_attrs`/`terminal_vel` in touched call sites.
smart_moves: Factored repeated parasol item null checks into `ensureUnkItem(gobj, cb)`, reducing duplicated callback wrappers for enter/exit hitlag behavior.
Separated common entry setup into `doEnter`, shared by grounded and aerial SpecialHi enter paths after their differing motion/velocity setup.
Separated collision logic into `doColl(gobj, cb)`, shared by start/end and ground/air variants while allowing different landing callbacks.
Used a `notUsingParasol` helper to make `ftPe_SpecialHi_NotActive` readable despite multiple motion-state ranges for Peach parasol, common item parasol, fall special, and landing fall special.
Preserved matching-only stack padding behind `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` with explicit `@todo Unused stack` notes instead of hiding matching artifacts in normal logic.
Improved struct semantics by naming Peach parasol item pointers and the SpecialHi item-kind motion variable, replacing raw offsets/integers with typed fields that match behavior.
Kept item ownership transitions explicit: existing held item can be stored as `parasol_gobj`, detached/reattached through item callbacks, and restored by `ftPe_8011D518`.
decomp_lessons: When matching fighter specials, first type the fighter vars and motion vars at the offsets the assembly uses; readable fields such as `fv.pe.unk_item_gobj`, `fv.pe.parasol_gobj`, and `mv.pe.specialhi.kind` make the C expression order much easier to control.

Shared ground/air variants often differ only in entry velocity or small physics details; extracting common `doEnter`, animation, IASA, and collision helpers can preserve matching while avoiding copied logic.
Accessory callbacks (`x21BC`) and hitlag callbacks (`x21D4`/`x21D8`) are central to item-backed moves; model them explicitly rather than treating them as anonymous function-pointer stores.
For item-backed specials, check item kind through `itGetKind` and store `ItemKind` in motion vars instead of raw constants where headers allow it.
Mechanical renames across fighter input/common attributes can be prerequisites for a match because new code needs stable semantic field names already used elsewhere.
Replacing expression macros with `static const` float values can help match CodeWarrior codegen in angle calculations; this PR switched `DEG_TO_RAD` uses to `deg_to_rad`.
Use `MUST_MATCH`-guarded unused stack arrays sparingly and document them as matching artifacts so future cleanup can revisit them without changing normal builds.
naming_conventions: New Peach SpecialHi C symbols follow the module prefix `ftPe_SpecialHi_` for static helpers/callbacks and exported callbacks such as `ftPe_SpecialHiStart_Anim`.
Grounded/aerial variants use paired names: `ftPe_SpecialHi_Enter` vs `ftPe_SpecialAirHi_Enter`, `Start` vs `End`, and suffixes `_Anim`, `_IASA`, `_Phys`, `_Coll`.
Unknown but behaviorally identified Peach fields were named `unk_item_gobj` and `parasol_gobj`, preserving uncertainty for one pointer while capturing item-object semantics.
Peach dat-attrs kept offset-style placeholder names (`x70`, `x74`, `x78`, etc.) when exact gameplay names were not established, but changed types from opaque bytes to floats/ints.
Motion variables use a nested union path `fp->mv.pe.specialhi.kind`, with `kind` typed as `ItemKind`.
Math constants were normalized to lowercase `deg_to_rad`/`rad_to_deg` static const names instead of all-caps macros.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted the assembly at `ftPe_Init.s` addresses around `8011D424` onward into C functions in `ftPe_SpecialHi.c`, shrinking the assembly file substantially while keeping the assembly object for remaining unmatched code.
Used `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` local byte arrays to reproduce unused stack space in several SpecialHi functions.
Used `fabs_inline`, `deg_to_rad`, and `M_PI_2` in steering/angle logic to match floating-point codegen while improving readability.

Maintained exact callback and item-call sequencing for parasol item setup/restoration: create with `it_802BDA64`, manage hitlag with `it_802BDBF8`/`it_802BDC18`, destroy/cleanup with `it_802BDB94`, and restore existing items with `it_8026BB20`/`it_8026B73C`.
Introduced helper functions but kept them static where the original labels were local, while exporting only functions declared in `ftPe_SpecialHi.h`.
Updated `obj_files.mk` rather than replacing the whole Peach init assembly object, allowing a partial asm-to-C split without forcing all of `ftPe_Init.s` to match at once.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice; no human review feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename Peach `ftPe_DatAttrs` fields `x70` through `x90` to gameplay-specific names.
Search for subsequent cleanup of `ftPe_SpecialHi_8011D424`, `ftPe_SpecialHi_8011DD8C`, and `ftPe_SpecialHi_8011E064` numeric suffixes.
Check later item PRs for real names of `it_802BDA64`, `it_802BDB94`, `it_802BDBF8`, `it_802BDC18`, and `it_802BDC38`.
Find PRs that remove `MUST_MATCH` unused stack arrays from Peach SpecialHi or normalize `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` style.
Search for later field naming around `fv.pe.unk_item_gobj` to determine whether it specifically represents the spawned Peach parasol item.
